subprocess_isolation.warning: claude binary not on PATH; running in same-session degraded mode. Subagents will execute in the calling agy session rather than isolated subprocesses. Pipeline kernel hooks (role-gate, ship-gate, phase-gate-precondition) still apply.

budget_cap.warning: no native budget cap on agy CLI; consider EVOLVE_RUN_TIMEOUT for an external bound. cost_blind:true is intentional for this infrastructure cycle — rollout cycle must add billing tap or turn-cap.

profile_permissions.warning: agy CLI has no profile-permission flags; subagents have access to whatever tools the calling agy session permits. Mitigation: pipeline kernel hooks and post-hoc artifact verification.

challenge_token.warning: challenge token verified post-hoc on artifact rather than embedded in subprocess profile

notes: Antigravity CLI (agy) supports non-interactive prompt mode via `agy -p` and `--dangerously-skip-permissions`. agy emits plain text only — no JSON envelope; the adapter appends a hardcoded zero-cost envelope (cost_blind:true) as the last line of STDOUT_LOG. HYBRID mode (when claude is on PATH) inherits all capabilities from claude.sh via subprocess isolation. DEGRADED mode (no agy, no claude) runs the pipeline in same-session execution; HYBRID vs DEGRADED distinction is meaningful for agy because HYBRID provides subprocess isolation, not just output quality. cost_blind:true is intentional for this infrastructure cycle — a rollout cycle must add a billing tap or turn-cap. See docs/architecture/platform-compatibility.md.
